**Carbohydrates** are a key energy source in **sport dog nutrition**, providing glucose for immediate fuel and supporting endurance during high-performance activities. They also spare amino acids from being diverted for energy, preserving muscle health. To maximize benefits, avoid excessive refined sugars and poorly digestible fillers. On ingredient labels, starch may appear as **brown rice, barley, or oats**, which supply sustained energy and aid recovery post-exercise by replenishing glycogen stores. Carbohydrates post-exercise help restore energy reserves efficiently. Direct carbohydrate levels are not displayed on labels due to regulatory standards focusing on **proximate analysis** (e.g., fiber, moisture) rather than total carbs. Among common sources, **rice** elicits the greatest blood glucose response, so diets containing rice or wheat should be blended with **fiber-rich ingredients** (e.g., oats, vegetables) to moderate spikes and support stable energy release.

After World War I, the *Governor R. M. McLane* was taken out of service in 1919 for engine repairs, including the installation of a new boiler. On December 3, 1919, while moored at the Locust Point Industrial Area in Baltimore, a fire nearly destroyed the vessel, which was adjacent to the Spedden shipyard. Plans to rebuild the *McLane* were funded by $10,000 in insurance money, and the Navy loaned the state the *USS Gov. R. M. McLane (SP-1328)* as a temporary replacement. The ship was later reconstructed by Spedden in 1920.

Construction began in **November 1968** but faced extreme cold delays. Completed by **March 1969** and named the **Walter J. Harold Lothrop Borden's military career began in 1893 when he joined the King's Canadian Hussars as a trooper at age 17. Commissioned as a lieutenant in 1897 with the 1st Battalion, Canadian Mounted Rifles, he served during the South African War. That same year, he was selected as a member of Queen Victoria's Diamond Jubilee Contingent, receiving the Queen Victoria Diamond Jubilee Medal. By 1899, he had risen to the rank of major, commanding his unit. His bravery in South Africa brought him to the attention of British commander-in-chief Field Marshal Lord Roberts, who commended him for gallant conduct during operations, including swimming the Vet River under fire in May 1900. Borden's militia service and leadership marked him as a distinguished officer before his death in combat later that year.

The injury required surgery, and Lack missed the remainder of the season. With Lack out the Wolves finished fifteenth in the league for fewest goals allowed. The previous season they allowed the fifth fewest goals in the league. Lack and back-up Matt Climie set a franchise record for lowest team goals against average. With the league's nineteenth best offense, the Wolves were unable to overcome the goals against them and finished fourth in their division. Thus missing the playoffs. It was the second time in three years that the Wolves missed the playoffs, but only the fourth time in franchise history. Despite missing the playoffs, Chicago finished second in league attendance.

During the Mamluk period, multi-story buildings occupied by rental apartments, known as a *rab‘* (plural *riba‘* or *urbu*), became common and continued into the Ottoman era. This type of housing was relatively unique to Cairo at the time, where apartments were often laid out as multi-story duplexes or triplexes. When attached to caravanserais (*wikalas*), the two lower floors served commercial and storage purposes, while the upper floors housed tenants. Even in such combinations, the apartment complexes had separate street entrances from the commercial areas below. The oldest partially-preserved example is the Wikala of Amir Qawsun, built before 1341. Residential buildings in Cairo were organized into close-knit neighborhoods called a *harat*, which often had gates that could be closed at night or during disturbances.

Prior to the development of the PSS, assessment of stress tended to focus on objective indicators (e.g., frequencies) of specific stressors (e.g., chronic illness, family loss, new family members). This tendency subsequently overlooks the influence an individual’s subjective interpretation of a stressor might have upon the experience of a stressor. Cohen et al. (1983) viewed the void of the subjective component in assessing stress as an unwanted quality and developed the PSS in response. Specifically, the PSS is based upon Lazarus's original transactional model of stress that argues the experience of a stressor is influenced by evaluations on the part of the person as to how well they can manage a stressor given their coping resources.

The climate at Purico is cold (mean temperatures −3 – −4 °C (27–25 °F)), the air thin because of the high elevation and ultraviolet radiation is high. There is little precipitation in the area (about 200 millimetres per year (7.9 in/year) on the upper parts of the shield, decreasing to less than 10 millimetres per year (0.39 in/year) close to the Salar de Atacama), which mainly happens during the summer months as a consequence of the South American monsoon. Snow also falls during winter but winter snow mainly evaporates while summer snow melts. This dry climate is due to the combined effects of the subtropical ridge, the Humboldt Current in the Pacific Ocean and the rain shadow exercised by the Andes, but it was in the past interrupted by wet periods.

The neuronal ceroid lipofuscinoses (NCLs) are a group of inherited neurodegenerative disorders with pathological phenotypes that auto fluorescent lipopigments present in neurons and other cell types. Over the past two decades, accumulating evidences indicates that NCLs are caused by mutations in eight different genes, including genes encoding several soluble proteins (cathepsin D, PPT1, and TPP1). Mutations of gene TPP1 result in late-infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis which is associated with the failure to degrade specific neuropeptides and a subunit of ATP synthase in the lysosome. Mutations in the TPP1 gene lead to late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis, a fatal neurodegenerative disease of childhood. It has been demonstrated that a single injection of intravitreal implantation of autologous bone marrow derived stem cells transduced with a TPP1 expression construct at an early stage in the disease progression could substantially inhibit the development of disease-related retinal function deficits and structural changes. This result implies that ex vivo gene therapy using autologous stem cells may be an effective means of achieving sustained delivery of therapeutic compounds to tissues such as the retina for which systemic administration would be ineffective.

**Morphology, classification and relatives**  
Salmonella virus P22 is a temperate, double-stranded DNA phage classified as a "lambdoid" phage due to its genetic regulation similarities with bacteriophage λ, including early operons and control regions for gene expression. However, its virion protein genes differ significantly from λ's. P22 has an icosahedral (T=7) head ~60 nm in diameter and a very short tail, placing it in the *Podoviridae* family. It shares genomic transcription patterns and life cycles with λ and other lambdoid phages, while its virion morphology and DNA homology in structural genes align more closely with short-tailed phages like L and ε34. Despite morphological similarities to other *Podoviridae* (e.g., T7, φ29), P22 lacks significant DNA sequence similarity with them. Its early left and right operons show mosaic structure, reflecting genetic exchanges within the lambdoid group.

Divorce rates have climbed in the European Union and the United States with increase in autonomous marriage rates. The lowest divorce rates in the world are in cultures with high rates of arranged marriages such as Amish culture of United States (1%), Hindus of India (3%), and Ultra-Orthodox Jews of Israel (7%). According to a 2012 study by Statistic Brain, 53.25% of marriages are arranged worldwide. The global divorce rate for arranged marriages was 6.3%, which could be an indicator for the success rate of arranged marriages. This has led scholars to ask if arranged marriages are more stable than autonomous marriages, and whether this stability matters. Others suggest that the low divorce rate may not reflect stability, rather it may reflect the difficulty in the divorce process and social ostracism to the individuals, who choose to live in a dysfunctional marriage rather than face the consequences of a divorce. P22 shares many similarities in genetic structure and regulation with bacteriophage λ. It is a temperate double stranded DNA phage as well as a lambdoid phage since it carries control of gene expression regions and early operons similar to those of bacteriophage λ. However, the genes which encode proteins that build the virion are different from those of bacteriophage λ. P22 has a 60 nm diameter icosahedral (T=7) virion head and a short tail. This virion morphology puts P22 in the formal Podoviridae group. Traditionally, P22 is associated with viruses with similar genomic transcription patterns and life cycles including bacteriophage λ and all the other lambdoid phages. However, this relatedness seems to be overestimated. Other relatives with similar short-tailed morphology and DNA homology in the protein genes of the virion include bacteriophages λ and Ε34. Many Podoviridae, for example phages T7 and Φ29, share few DNA similarities with P22, even though their virion morphologies are similar.

Arranged marriages are often associated with lower divorce rates compared to autonomous marriages, a trend observed alongside rising divorce rates in the European Union and the United States as autonomous marriages increase. Cultures with high rates of arranged marriages, such as Hindu communities in India (divorce ratio of 2.0 per 1,000 married individuals) and the Amish in the U.S. (where divorce is virtually nonexistent due to religious prohibition), report notably low divorce rates. Similarly, Ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel have a divorce rate of around 10%, lower than secular populations. Globally, 53.25% of marriages are arranged, with a divorce rate of just 6%, according to a 2012 Statistic Brain study. However, scholars question whether this stability reflects genuine marital satisfaction or societal pressure, particularly given concerns about forced unions and limited personal agency in arranged marriages.

**Education and community involvement** are critical in combating mosquito-borne diseases, particularly as arboviruses like dengue, Zika, and chikungunya expand into temperate regions due to the spread of *Aedes aegypti* and *Aedes albopictus* mosquitoes. Effective prevention requires integrated strategies, including surveillance, reducing breeding sites, and promoting personal protective measures. Communities are educated on eliminating standing water, using repellents, and recognizing symptoms to curb transmission. Outreach programs identify local risk factors—such as environmental or social gaps—and tailor interventions, like mobile clinics or school partnerships, to address them. By assessing which preventative measures communities are most likely to adopt, programs can enhance compliance. Health workers, local agencies, and schools collaborate in public education campaigns, emphasizing vector control and early detection to mitigate outbreaks.

In a shared memory multiprocessor system with a separate cache memory for each processor, multiple copies of shared data can exist: one in main memory and one in each processor's local cache. When a copy is modified, all other copies must reflect the change. Cache coherence is the discipline that ensures changes to shared operands are propagated throughout the system in a timely fashion, maintaining consistency across all caches and memory. This guarantees that writes become visible to all processors and are serialized.

Before the development of the Perceived Stress Scale (PSS) in 1983 by Sheldon Cohen and colleagues, stress assessment primarily focused on external stressors—specific events or experiences causing stress—without accounting for an individual's subjective perception of their ability to cope. Researchers often measured stressors like life events or environmental pressures but overlooked the psychological appraisal of stress, a key component of Lazarus and Folkman's transactional model of stress. Cohen et al. viewed this omission as a limitation, arguing that stress is shaped by dynamic interactions between appraisal and coping. The PSS, based on this model, evaluates perceived stress by assessing how unpredictable, uncontrollable, and overwhelming life feels, emphasizing the role of cognitive and emotional responses in shaping stress experiences.

Multi-story buildings occupied by rental apartments, known as a rab' (plural ribā' or urbu), became common in the Mamluk period and continued to be a feature of the city's housing during the later Ottoman period. This type of housing was a relatively unique to Cairo at the time. These apartments were often laid out as multi-story duplexes or triplexes. They were sometimes attached to caravanserais (wikalas) where the two lower floors were for commercial and storage purposes and the upper floors above were rented out to tenants. Even in the case of these wikala-rab' combinations, the apartment complexes had their own street entrances separate from the commercial complex below. The oldest partially-preserved example of this type of structure is the Wikala of Amir Qawsun, which was built before 1341, but multiple later examples have survived from later centuries, either as part of caravanserais or as independent buildings. Residential buildings in Cairo were in turn organized into close-knit neighbourhoods called a harat, which in many cases had gates that could be closed off at night or during disturbances.

Nagumo’s force, consisting of five carriers and four battleships, aimed to destroy Allied shipping and naval bases. Despite being outnumbered and outclassed, Somerville hoped to launch a night-time torpedo bomber attack. On 4 April, *Warspite*’s fast group set sail to intercept the Japanese, detecting their attack on the cruisers *Cornwall* and *Dorsetshire* before withdrawing to Addu Atoll and later to Kilindini on the East African coast to protect convoy routes. The Japanese, mistakenly believing *Warspite* was still in Sydney, ordered an attack there.

Cephaloridine is a cephalosporin compound with pyridinium-1-ylmethyl and 2-thienylacetamido side groups. **Acute postoperative endophthalmitis** caused by *Staphylococcus lugdunensis* was reported in a prospective, case-control study conducted at university-affiliated hospitals in Japan (approved by Kyoto Prefectural University of Medicine Hospital, #RBMR-C-864). Among 157,653 cataract surgeries, 38 cases of acute endophthalmitis were identified (incidence: 0.024%), with symptoms (pain, hypopyon, vitritis) presenting at a median of 6.5 days post-surgery. Severe vitreous opacities caused significant visual loss in three cases. Initial treatment included intravitreal ceftazidime (0.2 mg) and systemic antibiotics (vancomycin, ceftazidime), with corticosteroids and cycloplegics. Pars plana vitrectomy (PPV) was required in severe cases (exact number unspecified). Chronic inflammation or cystoid macular edema (CME) worsened prognosis in three patients. Microbiological diagnosis involved vitreous sampling (21G/23G needle or vitrector) with stains and culture media (e.g., for *S. epidermidis*, identified in 53.8% of isolates in similar studies).

**Migration**  
After Brexit, the UK regained control over immigration from the EU and EEA, ending the principle of free movement, which had allowed EU/EEA citizens to live, work, and study in the UK without restrictions. The *European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018* retained EU law, including free movement rules, as UK law until repealed. The *Immigration and Social Security Co-ordination (EU Withdrawal) Bill* formally ended free movement after the transition period (31 December 2020), paving the way for a points-based system prioritizing skills over nationality. The government’s 2018 white paper proposed a single skills-based system, with a £30,000 salary threshold for skilled workers. EU/EEA citizens already in the UK before 2020 could secure their status via the *EU Settlement Scheme (EUSS)*, launched in 2019, with applications due by June 2021. Irish citizens (including dual British-Irish nationals) were exempt from applying. In a no-deal scenario, EU arrivals before 2021 could have applied for temporary leave to remain until December 2023. The new system aims to align immigration with economic needs while integrating EU migrants already resident under the EUSS.

**Artifact recovery and conservation** in underwater archaeology involves stabilizing artifacts to manage water removal and prevent deterioration. Two methods are used: careful drying or replacing water with an inert medium, as seen with *The Mary Rose*. Metals and glass from saltwater require stabilization due to salt absorption or metal leaching. In-situ conservation of underwater structures is possible but must account for the site's dynamic nature. Intrusive investigations or artifact removal may cause scouring, exposing the site to further degradation. Scouring accelerates deterioration by destabilizing the surrounding sediment and exposing vulnerable materials.

In March, Somerville received intelligence indicating the Japanese Fast Carrier Strike Force was heading towards the Indian Ocean and he relocated his base to Addu Atoll in the Maldives. Vice Admiral Chuichi Nagumo used five carriers and four battleships in the Indian Ocean raid a naval sortie into the Indian Ocean in April, attacking Allied shipping and bases in the Indian Ocean and the Bay of Bengal. Somerville's fleet was outnumbered and outclassed but he hoped to get close enough to launch a night-time torpedo bomber attack. Warspite's fast group set sail to intercept on 4 April, detecting the Japanese attack on the cruisers Cornwall and Dorsetshire and later, a scouting aircraft from the cruiser Tone. The Japanese believed that she was still in Sydney and ordered the Attack on Sydney Harbour.

The simplest method involves filling the area with large amounts of heavy rock and/or cement, then filling with clay and dirt until the desired height is reached. The process is called "infilling" and the material used to fill the space is generally called "infill". Draining of submerged wetlands is often used to reclaim land for agricultural use. Deep cement mixing is used typically in situations in which the material displaced by either dredging or draining may be contaminated and hence needs to be contained. Land dredging is also another method of land reclamation. It is the removal of sediments and debris from the bottom of a body of water. It is commonly used for maintaining reclaimed land masses as sedimentation, a natural process, fills channels and harbors.

Acute postoperative endophthalmitis caused by S. lugdunensis is infrequently reported in clinical studies. Five cases of acute postcataract surgery endophthalmitis were taken from a multicenter prospective study conducted in four university-affiliated hospitals in France (2004 to 2005). These cases were characterized by severe ocular inflammation occurring with a mean delay of 7.6 days after cataract surgery, severe visual loss (hand motions or less in three cases), and dense infiltration of the vitreous. Each of these patients was initially treated by using a standard protocol with intravitreal (vancomycin and ceftazidime), systemic, and topical antibiotics. Given the severity of the endophthalmitis, though bacteria were sensitive to intravitreal antibiotics, pars plana vitrectomy was needed in four cases. The final visual prognosis was complicated by severe retinal detachment in three cases. The microbiological diagnosis was reached by using conventional cultures with specific biochemical tests and eubacterial PCR amplification followed by direct sequencing.

Artifacts recovered from underwater sites need stabilization to manage the process of removal of water and conservation. The artifact either needs to be dried carefully, or the water replaced with some inert medium (as in the case of The Mary Rose). The cold season in the United States is marked by nor'easters—extratropical cyclones that bring heavy wintry precipitation, primarily between December and March. These storms typically form between Georgia and New Jersey before tracking northeast along the coast. While nor'easters deliver snow to the Northeast, the Appalachian Mountains largely shield cities like New York and Philadelphia from lake-effect snow, which instead impacts regions downwind of Lakes Erie and Ontario in New York, where long fetches allow significant accumulation. Lake-effect snow can persist into early spring in upstate New York, while ocean-effect snows occur near coastal areas like Eastern Massachusetts and Cape Cod. Bay-effect snows, such as those near Chesapeake Bay, fall when cold air aloft meets moisture from warmer waters.

The cost of CO₂ emissions avoided through calcium looping (Ca-looping) is lower than that of oxyfuel combustion, with estimates around **€20–30 per tonne** compared to **€30–45 per tonne** for oxyfuel post-2030. This cost advantage stems from Ca-looping's **reduced oxygen demand** in calciners (~20% of oxyfuel requirements), lowering air separation unit (ASU) costs by **~30%**. Unlike oxyfuel, which captures CO₂ only from oxy-fired combustion, Ca-looping captures CO₂ from **both the oxy-fired calciner and the main combustor**. Figure 3 in sensitivity analyses shows Ca-looping's cost is most sensitive to **sorbent activity, fuel price, and plant efficiency**, with these variables dominating overall capture costs. The process also benefits from **integrated heat recovery**, further reducing energy penalties compared to standalone oxyfuel systems.

**Ear avulsions** occur when the external ear is traumatically detached, often due to its exposed position on the head. The most common causes include bite injuries, road accidents, and assaults. Human bites can lead to avulsions due to the high-energy trauma and variable anatomy of the ear. A partially avulsed ear may be reattached using meticulous multi-layer repair or staged repair, such as burying the segment in a postauricular pocket for revascularization. For complete avulsions, microvascular surgery is preferred but has lower success rates due to damaged vessels. If reattachment fails, reconstruction with rib cartilage, implants, or 3D-printed frameworks is an option. Prostheses, made by anaplastologists from medical-grade silicone, offer an alternative.

The neuronal ceroid lipofuscinoses (NCLs) are a group of fatal, inherited lysosomal storage disorders characterized by neurodegeneration, neuronal loss, and accumulation of autofluorescent lipopigments (ceroid lipofuscin) in cells. At least 10 genes are implicated in NCLs, with some encoding soluble lysosomal enzymes like *TPP1*. Mutations in *TPP1* cause tripeptidyl peptidase 1 (TPP1) deficiency, leading to late-infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (NCL2; CLN2 disease). This results in impaired lysosomal degradation and progressive neurological decline. Presymptomatic treatment with cerliponase alfa (enzyme replacement therapy) has been shown to inhibit disease progression. Additionally, ex vivo gene therapy using autologous stem cells transduced with *TPP1* restored retinal function in canine models, suggesting its potential as a therapeutic strategy for systemic delivery.

The concept of **ambivalent prejudice** was applied to immigrants and refugees by **Matsuo and McIntyre**, who described attitudes toward them as conflicting, shaped by egalitarianism and the Protestant work ethic (PWE). They identified two contrasting perceptions: immigrants as **hardworking** (PWE-aligned) or as **threats to resources** (egalitarianism-violating). Using an **online survey**, they tested these values by having participants complete questions on **immigration attitudes, PWE, and egalitarianism**, measured on a **Likert scale**. Their findings revealed that ambivalent attitudes stem from **competing value systems**, with prejudice decreasing only when **contact with refugees reduced perceived threat**, supporting contact theory. This aligns with broader research showing that **mixed conceptions of national identity** heighten attitudinal variability (Contexts 1, 2, 6).

Azzam began his career in Italy in the late 1950s, with his band, singing in Italian and in English. In 1960, he released two songs in France that were influenced by oriental music, "Ya Mustafa" ("Chérie je t'aime, chérie je t'adore, como la salsa del pomodoro") and "Fais-moi du couscous, chéri". The same year, he received the Grand Prix du Disque for the song "Viens à Juan les Pins".

The **2021 West Midlands mayoral election** was held to elect the mayor of the **West Midlands**, the UK's second most populous city-region after Greater London. The position was created following a **2015 devolution deal**, with the first mayor elected in **May 2017** under the **Cities and Local Government Devolution Act 2016**, which mandated metro mayors for combined authorities seeking additional powers. The inaugural term lasted **three years**, with subsequent elections held **every four years**. The **2017 election** saw Conservative **Andy Street** defeat Labour's **Sion Simon** by a narrow margin. Street sought re-election in 2021 after winning his first full term in 2020.

Robert Heptinstall was born in Keswick, England, and earned his medical degree from Charing Cross Hospital Medical School in 1943. During World War II, he served in the Royal Army Medical Corps, parachuting into Japanese-occupied Thailand with Force 136 to aid prisoners of war. After the war, he studied pathology at St. Mary's Hospital in London before moving to the U.S., where he became Pathologist-in-Chief at Johns Hopkins Hospital.

**Ludovike Simanowiz** (née Reichenbach; 21 February 1759 – [death date]) was a German portrait painter born in Schorndorf. Her father, a military officer, was stationed in Ludwigsburg around 1764, where she grew up alongside Friedrich Schiller, whose talent her mother, a cousin of Schiller's, first recognized. Schiller became her lifelong friend.

Bonnie Devine was born in Toronto and is an off-reserve member of the Serpent River First Nation (Anishinaabe/Ojibwa) in Northern Ontario. She graduated from the Ontario College of Art and Design (OCAD) in sculpture and installation in 1997 and earned a Master of Fine Arts from York University in 1999. She has taught at York University, Queen's University, and the Centre for Indigenous Theatre. Joining OCAD University as a full-time instructor in 2008, she became the Founding Chair of its Indigenous Visual Culture Program.

Coastal extratropical cyclones, known as nor'easters, bring a bulk of the wintry precipitation to the region during the cold season as they track parallel to the coastline, forming along the natural temperature gradient of the Gulf stream before moving up the coastline. The Appalachian Mountains largely shield New York City and Philadelphia from picking up any lake-effect snow, though ocean-effect snows are possible near Cape Cod. The Finger Lakes of New York are long enough for lake-effect precipitation. Lake-effect snow from the Finger Lakes occurs in upstate New York until those lakes freeze over. Bay-effect snows fall downwind of Delaware Bay, Chesapeake Bay, and Massachusetts Bay when the basic criteria are met. Ocean effect snows are possible downwind of the Gulf Stream across the Southeast.

The station began operation on March 5, 1930, as WFDV, operating on 1370 kHz with a power of 100 W. On June 26, 1931, the station was permitted to change its frequency to 1310 kHz. By April 1935 its callsign had become WRGA. On May 16, 1948, WRGA changed its frequency from 1490 kHz to 1470 kHz with a concurrent increase in power from 250 W to 5 KW.
